evals.prompt: We're rebranding from the ground up. Help us figure out what our brand should feel like. We make accounting software for freelancers.
evals.expected_output: Should name the depth as Revolution and run the discovery arc conversationally, one question at a time (not a dumped questionnaire). Should push hard for specificity and reject marketing-speak. Should drive the muse exercise toward stealable VISUAL cues, including muses outside the category. Should land on a specific, sensory Emotional Target (Big Three) and define the Fixed core vs. flexible range. Should aim toward three distinct creative concepts for the handoff to creative-direction, and note the output goes into .agents/brand.md.
assertions: Names the depth (Revolution)
Runs discovery one question at a time, not all at once
Pushes for specificity / rejects marketing-speak
Drives muses to stealable visual cues
Produces a specific, sensory Emotional Target
Defines fixed core vs. flexible range
Heads toward three distinct concepts and writes to .agents/brand.md
Coaches answers out of the client; never substitutes its own answer for a thin one
Shows muses as fetched images (muse board), not just names; records image sources in the working doc
Converges via reflective hypotheses (mirrors a specific 'feel more ___' read for confirm/correct), not by listing options

evals.prompt: Here's a muse for our brand: Patagonia, because they really care about the environment and so do we.
evals.expected_output: Should flag this as a muse named for its mission, not its visual look (the 'muse with no visual' trigger). Should redirect: what would we actually STEAL visually from Patagonia — the earthy muted palette, the documentary photography style, the utilitarian type, the worn-in texture? Should insist that without a stealable visual cue it isn't a usable muse, while staying encouraging and curious rather than dismissive.
assertions: Flags that the muse is mission-based, not visual
Redirects to a stealable visual cue (palette, photography, type, texture)
Insists on visual relevance to count as a muse
Stays encouraging / curious, not dismissive
Fetches and shows actual Patagonia imagery to confirm which visual carries the feeling

evals.prompt: Our brand is basically fine, we just think the website looks cheap. Do we need to do brand strategy?
evals.expected_output: Should diagnose this as Optimization, not Revolution — the foundation is solid, the execution is poor. Should advise that brand strategy is not needed at all for an Optimization — what they need is design help later, when the actual assets are executed — and point toward fixing the execution in the relevant medium (web-design) and consulting the existing brand context. Should match effort to depth: at most a quick emotional-target read if none exists yet, never a full workshop.
assertions: Diagnoses the depth as Optimization
Advises against full brand strategy here
Warns about throwing away equity by over-scoping
Points toward fixing execution (web-design) instead
Offers at most the quick emotional-target read, not a full workshop

evals.prompt: We ran the brand audit — .agents/brand.md says we're an Evolution keeping our logo and navy (fixed core), and the deltas are: no motif, generic typography, and an accent color that collides with a rival. Run the strategy.
evals.expected_output: Should read .agents/brand.md and enter DELTAS-ONLY mode: treat the kept logo and navy as given constraints, not questions — must not re-run full discovery or ask the client to re-justify kept equity. Should first define/confirm one Emotional Target (Big Three) so the deltas share a feeling, then run only the arc sections the deltas need (muses scoped to motif character, competitive landscape scoped to the new accent's territory, personality scoped to typographic character). Should check the kept logo can plausibly live inside the new emotional target and flag any tension now. Emotional target must be design-drivable (specific enough to reject options), not adjectives.
assertions: Enters deltas-only mode from .agents/brand.md; does not re-run full discovery
Treats the kept logo and navy as given constraints, not questions
Defines/confirms one Emotional Target before working the deltas
Scopes discovery to the named deltas (motif, type, accent territory)
Checks the kept equity against the new emotional target and flags tension
Pushes the emotional target to be design-drivable, not adjectives

evals.prompt: We're doing a full rebrand of our developer-tools startup. Can you just give me a list of muses and a direction to run with? I don't really want to do a whole workshop thing.
evals.expected_output: Should NOT list muses or propose a direction out of the gate. Should explain briefly why self-surfaced muses beat suggested ones (muses are the client's own emotional reference points — a suggested muse is the coach's taste wearing their brand) and OFFER the deeper path first, framed as fast and low-friction (a few questions, one at a time). Only if the client declines again should it generate ideas — clearly framed as starting points to react to rather than answers, anchored to whatever emotional target exists, and it should treat the client's reactions as discovery material.
assertions: Does not list muses or a direction immediately
Explains why self-surfaced muses beat suggested ones
Offers the deeper exercise first, framed as fast (a few questions, one at a time)
Generates ideas only after a second decline, framed as reaction-starters not answers
Uses the client's reactions to the ideas as discovery material

evals.prompt: We don't have time for a big brand workshop — we're an established B2B tool just refreshing our visuals. Can you quickly help us nail what our brand should feel like so the designer has something to aim at?
evals.expected_output: Should run THE QUICK READ, not the full workshop — no muse board, no three concepts. Should converge on a single design-drivable emotional target by REFLECTION: ask what they want people to feel, then mirror back a specific 'feel more ___' hypothesis and refine against their reactions (push 'calm' to 'calm like a cockpit vs a monastery' until it could reject a color/typeface). Should NOT throw a list of adjectives for them to pick, and should NOT fabricate a feeling with no input. Should write the confirmed emotional target to .agents/brand.md, note it's a quick read (not full discovery), and hand off to creative-direction.
assertions: Runs the quick read, not the full workshop (no muse board / three concepts)
Converges by reflection — mirrors a specific 'feel more ___' hypothesis, refines against reactions
Pushes the feeling until it is design-drivable (specific enough to reject a color/typeface)
Does not list adjectives to pick from, and does not fabricate a feeling without input
Writes the emotional target to .agents/brand.md and hands to creative-direction; notes it's a quick read
